@khemaridh I believe that burnout is propagated by the idea that we *should* be different to who or where we are (ie, that we aren’t OK/are wrong as we are), and basing our life choices & values on external ideals that disconnect us from-- & DENY-- our deepest truths. 

synonym: shame 🫶 https://t.co/3ywcsqEE5S

we yearn. we learn. we wander. 

when we can see our yearnings with new eyes, a life of yearning is a life of wonder. 

when we can feel the depth & breadth of our yearnings, we come alive. 

a life of yearning is a life of learning is a life of love: there's always more.

even--or especially!--if we’re terrified of it, we yearn to be able to trust. we yearn to be able to live & love with an open heart. we yearn to-- & for!-- for love. we yearn to embody & embrace the love that we already are. we yearn to come home to love & to ourselves.

patterns repeat themselves until there is space for them to be fully felt & alchemised. we heal in relationships because we are social creatures who deeply need-- & NEED TO BE ABLE--to rely on one another.

crucially, something was missing that we couldn't speak about bc (aside from safety etc) we didn't have the words for it. when children feel that void, they oscillate b/t yearning for it to be filled & shutting it (themselves) down entirely to survive. adults repeat this pattern.

for most, the pain of being unmet dates back to childhood. it isn't necessarily that something is/was overtly “wrong” in or w our family of origin (although that might be true). it's that something wasn't there. something was missing.

while you need me, I can feel important.

while I need you, I can feel close to you.

truth bomb: 

codependent relationships are perfectly functioning relationships.

they function BECAUSE both parties are receiving something they want & rely on. 

there is deep wisdom in this.

in codependent relationships, love is only safe if it's conditional. 

when love is conditional, it's more akin to a transaction than love. 

you may not be paying with $, but you're “paying” for something you want/need in ways that are measured (+ likely also defined) by the OP.

I had to learn how to see the beauty in pain without drowning in it. I had to learn how to feel the joy in loss to be able to open myself to more love, hope & possibility. I had to learn how to feel & embody strength in softness. I had to learn how to be with & nurture myself.

while you yearn for me to be different, you can't love me, either. & it's also one level deeper than that: I can’t feel your love. I feel your judgement instead.
while I yearn for you to be different, I can’t truly love you. a part of me doesn't want to; I am intrinsically resistant.

if you can't see me,I will not reveal myself to you. I will not put myself at risk; I will not cause myself unnecessary pain. this isn't me hiding; it's me being smart. it's me recognising & honouring where you're at, and attuning myself to our collective needs without judgement.

our “baggage” is not a sign that something is wrong with us. it’s the manifestation of all of the things that we are not yet ready or available to see & feel. there is deep wisdom in that, too. we always know the best thing to do.

I’ve heard the phrase “we all come with baggage” a lot, and it no longer resonates with me. I'm yet to meet a person who “comes with baggage” who doesn't also have unspoken (& therefore intrinsically unmet) needs.
an unspoken requirement for expansive love is being able to meet the other person where they are & recognise that they are different to (& no less important than) you.

it isn't [just] that I want to control my own narrative; it's that I want this [expression] not to be distorted. I want there to be an energetic cleanliness to it. I want to model what it’s like to live with your heart open. I feel deeply & I feel everything. I have no regrets.

I also want to know what I--what this--mean/s to you. how this touches you. what this evokes in you. I desire kindness & gentleness. I desire to be able to be as soft & sad as I am Everything Else. I desire to be able to be that way without it becoming something other to itself.

this started off as a love letter to my insecurities, but it's become so much deeper. 

what do I want right now? 

simply to know that I am seen. simply to know that I am loved. simply to know that I am safe. 

if it feels good for you: gentle messages of appreciation & love.

we are all soft & hard at the same time. we have all loved & lost. we all know pain. we all know what it’s like to hide-- or be afraid of-- who really are. and, of course, we all know what it’s like to judge.

I am still grieving a wound associated with my “big personality”. I took up too much space because I learned to speak. I was a force of nature & a force for love before I ever claimed myself as such. the difference was that I was bitterly unhappy.

there's a softness to all of this. sometimes I wonder whether, if you saw my softness, you'd be able to hold it. I want you to be able to see me-as-me, not your projections. I know we are mirrors, and... the game continues.
right now, I want to cradle myself. I want to tell myself what a great job I'm doing. I want to hold myself like a big baby. & I want to be held. I yearn simply to be held.

power is not synonymous with invincibility. or rather: power is not synonymous with insensitivity. I am a sensitive soul. I am human, like everyone else. I enjoy expressing myself. the purest expression is the present moment. everything else is a story.

I want to use words carefully. when I speak about activation, I'm speaking about a state of being where I’m on edge & jarred. hypervigilant. crumbling. sad. caught between fight & flight. the slow freeze. in these moments, it feels good to breathe. it's what I need.that, & a hug.

I want to talk abt little-big reminders of my earliest memories: being rejected, an outcast, unwanted & misunderstood-- or perhaps just feeling this way. either way, I hold these memories in my body, and I notice how much discussions of in-group & outgroup politics activate me.

I want to talk about themes I'm noticing rather than specifics. I want to talk about experiences I'm alchemising bc it helps me to anchor any shifts. I want to talk about the little-big pull within me that wants to be friends w everyone.

there is something so strange & so lovely about existing so publicly here. there's something so strange & so lovely about coexisting too. there's something so strange & so lovely about noticing the way attention can flow in different directions. I want to cradle my tender heart.
